description: Weekly rotating menu announcements featuring new dishes with seasonal themes, detailed ingredient descriptions, and date ranges. Highlights premium or trending items like Dubai chocolate variants with visual appeal and specificity.
structural_recipe: Lead with seasonal opener (e.g., 'Spring is here') paired with casual emoji. State menu availability dates clearly (e.g., 'Mar 23–28'). Use visual separators (emojis, dashes). List featured dishes with color-coded hearts or symbols. Include detailed descriptions of ingredients, textures, and fillings. Emphasize premium components (Dubai chocolate, kataifi, pistachio). Use both Arabic and English or code-switch naturally.
why_it_works: Saudi F&B audiences respond to transparency about ingredients and sourcing. Weekly rotation creates urgency and repeat-visit motivation. Premium ingredient callouts (Dubai chocolate, pistachio) signal quality and justify pricing. Seasonal framing builds emotional connection and aligns with cultural dining occasions.
transplantation_caveats: Ensure halal status is clear or implied through ingredient transparency
Avoid alcohol references or non-halal ingredients in descriptions
Verify date ranges align with local holidays and prayer times
Premium pricing language may require careful calibration for different market segments
cultural_constraints: Must respect Islamic dietary laws in all ingredient mentions
Consider Friday/weekend dining patterns when setting promotion dates
Premium positioning should avoid appearing exclusionary
